memory.summary: I see a tempting chance to relax now, but my long-term planning horizon pulls me toward the quick chore first. Starting the term project work feels safer for our future success.

desires: Not disrupt the flow of our group dinner tonight with a sudden burst of energy or a complaint about my fatigue.
Maintain my reputation as someone who handles long-term obligations (the term project) without last-minute panic, even if it means ignoring the instant gratification of the chore right now.
Ensure I don't appear unreliable to cohort_3 by quietly finishing the necessary groundwork before anyone asks for an update.

goals.description: Arrive at the group dinner with zero visible signs of fatigue or urgency to preserve group harmony and my reliable reputation.
goals.rationale: Affiliation is my highest value; disrupting the flow for a trivial instant win would risk closeness with cohort_3. Avoidant conflict style means I will suppress the urge to vent about stress rather than address it directly.

goals.description: Quietly draft the foundational outline for the term project during the afternoon block so the groundwork is done before anyone asks, ensuring long-term stability without creating friction now.
goals.rationale: My deliberate time horizon dictates investing in future payoff (Thursday deadline) over immediate gratification. However, since the 'chore' offers an instant win that doesn't serve the relationship or the long-term goal, deferring it aligns with avoiding unnecessary friction while still honoring the need to appear competent later.

agent.last_reasoning: Investing now secures the Thursday deadline without last-minute stress that could ruin dinner with cohort_3. Skipping this trivial instant win avoids future friction; I'll join the group relaxed, preserving harmony.
